(German pronunciation: [[ˈzɛntlɪŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Sendling.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Sendling.wav")) is a borough of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"). It is located south-west of the city centre and spans the city boroughs Sendling and [Sendling-Westpark](/wiki/Sendling-Westpark "Sendling-Westpark"). Sendling is subdivided into Obersendling, Mittersendling, and Untersendling. Untersendling and Mittersendling are located in the borough of Sendling, and Obersendling is located in the borough of [Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-Fürstenried-Solln](/wiki/Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln "Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-Fürstenried-Solln").

Retrieved on 22 January 2015. Aerial view of the Market Hall area (view to the east). A legend is available on the picture's page.

**Sendling** (German pronunciation: [[ˈzɛntlɪŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Sendling.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Sendling.wav")) is a borough of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"). It is located south-west of the city centre and spans the city boroughs Sendling and [Sendling-Westpark](/wiki/Sendling-Westpark "Sendling-Westpark"). Sendling is subdivided into Obersendling, Mittersendling, and Untersendling. Untersendling and Mittersendling are located in the borough of Sendling, and Obersendling is located in the borough of [Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-Fürstenried-Solln](/wiki/Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-F%C3%BCrstenried-Solln "Thalkirchen-Obersendling-Forstenried-Fürstenried-Solln").

## Overview

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sendling&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

Sendling is mainly a residential quarter, with shops and businesses straddling the *Plinganserstraße* around the historical core of Sendling. It is a multicultural quarter, with one of the largest rates of foreigners among the population. The proposed site for the new [mosque in Sendling](/wiki/Mosque_in_Sendling "Mosque in Sendling") is located at *Gotzinger Platz*, opposite *St. Korbinian* Church. Supposedly, this neighborhood boasts the best falafels in town, located in the Valleystrasse.

*Harras*, an urban square near the historical centre of Sendling, is the busiest square of the borough. A number of shops and businesses line the square; *Plinganserstraße*, an important traffic artery running from north to south, skirts Harras in the east. A railway station, Harras Station, is located nearby, serviced by [U-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_U-Bahn "Munich U-Bahn") metro and [S-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_S-Bahn "Munich S-Bahn") suburban trains. Harras is the business point in Munich-South. There are many shops, working places and two of the biggest schools in Munich: [Dante-Gymnasium](/w/index.php?title=Dante-Gymnasium&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dante-Gymnasium (page does not exist)") and Klenze-Gymnasium.

The Westpark is a large recreational park located in the west of the borough. It was laid out for the [International Gardening Exhibition](/w/index.php?title=International_Gardening_Exhibition&action=edit&redlink=1 "International Gardening Exhibition (page does not exist)") that took place in Munich in 1983. Munich's main traffic artery, the [Mittlerer Ring](/wiki/Mittlerer_Ring "Mittlerer Ring"), cuts right through the park and divides it into an eastern and a western park.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Sendling is served by the U-Bahn metro lines 3 and 6 as well as the S-Bahn suburban trains 7, 20 and 27. Harras Station is served by some regional train services, as well.

The [Wholesale Market Munich](/wiki/Wholesale_Market_Munich "Wholesale Market Munich"), in the eastern part of the borough, is Europe's third-largest grocery market as well as the borough's largest single employer.

In the very northeast of Sendling the [Alte Utting](/wiki/Alte_Utting "Alte Utting") is located, a nightlife spot and attraction that became known far beyond Munich's city borders.

## Sendling's Night of Murder

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sendling&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Sendling's Night of Murder")]

Main article: [Sendling's Night of Murder](/wiki/Sendling%27s_Night_of_Murder "Sendling's Night of Murder")

Historically, Sendling is known for the *Sendlinger Mordweihnacht* ("Sendling's Night of Murder"), a massacre of rebelling peasants which took place there in 1705.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sendling&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Education")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Sendling&action=edit&section=). *(January 2015)* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/26/Lyc%C3%A9e_Jean_Renoir.JPG/250px-Lyc%C3%A9e_Jean_Renoir.JPG)](/wiki/File:Lyc%C3%A9e_Jean_Renoir.JPG)

Secondary campus of the [Lycée Jean Renoir](/wiki/Lyc%C3%A9e_Jean_Renoir_(Munich) "Lycée Jean Renoir (Munich)")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/79/Japanische_Internationale_Schule_M%C3%BCnchen_01.JPG/250px-Japanische_Internationale_Schule_M%C3%BCnchen_01.JPG)](/wiki/File:Japanische_Internationale_Schule_M%C3%BCnchen_01.JPG)

[Japanische Internationale Schule München](/wiki/Japanische_Internationale_Schule_M%C3%BCnchen "Japanische Internationale Schule München")

The *[Lycée Jean Renoir](/wiki/Lyc%C3%A9e_Jean_Renoir_(Munich) "Lycée Jean Renoir (Munich)")*, a French international school, maintains its secondary school campus in Sendling.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The [Japanische Internationale Schule München](/wiki/Japanische_Internationale_Schule_M%C3%BCnchen "Japanische Internationale Schule München"), a Japanese international school, is located in Sendling.[[3]](#cite_note-DieWelt-3)
